imperial stout.name: imperial stout
description: Finely aged Imperial Stout, canned. Good for your taste buds and your morale.

keycard.name: keycard
description: Keycard of the area manager.
It has a magnetic stripe.

excalibur.name: excalibur
description: Megacorp's flagship product, crafted as a perfect replica of King Arthur's legendary blade.
While richly adorned and flawlessly balanced, it is far too sharp to be a mere HEMA training weapon.
Truly a blade fit for the King of Knights.
It can't be sold to peasants.

keypad.name: keypad
description: A security keypad.
It's mounted on the wall and controls access to the nearby door.
The display says:"Swipe the badge and sort the colors".

blue note.name: blue note
description: A blue note with a scribbled code:

red note.name: red note
description: A red note with a scribbled code:

button.name: button
description: It is labeled: "Treasury".

winged button.name: winged button
description: This wing shaped button is labeled: "Project W2".

blue button.name: blue button
description: It is labeled: "Blue vault"

red button.name: red button
description: It is labeled: "Red vault."

laptop.name: laptop
description: A trusty, no frills, laptop.

power armor.name: power armor
description: Latest, secret, creation of Megacorp, it outclasses all previous products.
The power armor is the pinnacle of modern technology, featuring immense protection and durability.
It's the first armor capable of withstanding anti-matter rifle shots.
It integrates an exoskeleton and a neural augmentation that improves strength, speed and reaction times to inhuman levels.
Thanks to nanotechnology, everything is miniaturized and it looks like a true Renaissance tournament plate armor.
The design is incredibly elaborate, with etching and gilding techniques used on each plate, complete with a close helm, gauntlets and every necessary piece.
It is modeled after a suit of armor, now preserved in the Wallace Collection, supposedly made for Alfonso II D’Este, Duke of Ferrara.
The original employed essentially every decorative technique possible and was almost certainly the work of the virtuoso goldsmith-armourer Lucio Marliani, known as Piccinino.
Truly a work of art in all senses.

invoice.name: invoice
description: This is an invoice, the expense is called "Extra Security"
It's quite high, paid in advance and refers to only this week, but it costs more than the average yearly wage.
Reading that, you wonder if this was really the best time to come at MEGACORP.

post-it.name: post-it
description: Attached to the broken training dummy, you found this post it. It says:
"Take to W2 area."

letter.name: letter
description: In a closet in the changing room, you found this letter. It says:
"They said we should have observed her and taken note."
"But she just sliced through it like a hot knife through butter!"
"She is still unsurpassed, by far. A long way to go to achieve W2 supremacy."
"She is so scary. And her demeanor makes it surreal!"

circular.name: circular
description: On a seat in the main hall, you found this circular.
Someone forgot it here, since it's classified. It says:
"Observe her and learn as much as you can."
"We put her on guard duty at the Hall of Mirrors."
"She agreed to that, saying she'll consider herself free to leave if she finds someone worthy."
"I'm not entirely sure what that means, she speaks in a rather quaint way.
"But that clause halved the cost and that was clear."
"Anyway, don't make her angry and be accommodating. Survival first."

directive.name: directive
description: In the lab you found this directive. It says:
"Collect all information you can."
"Filter and process it, then send it to the W2 area."
"I don't need to remind to you that WelkinWeb is our most ambitious project, and it will make us the number one corporation in the world."
"Pay particular attention to her data.
"She's here for just a week."
"It might be a small step, but the ultimate goal is big."

torn page.name: torn page
description: In the gothic lab, you found this torn page in a trash can. It probably belonged to a diary. It says:
"WelkinWeb? W2? Maybe WW2 is more appropriate! Or, even worse, WW3."
"Because this will wreak havoc like a World War. Or worse."
"WW4 won't even be fought with sticks and stones, because there won't be anyone left!"
"But maybe I am exaggerating. I must calm down and keep the process under control."
"I must stay involved and endure, precisely because it's dangerous."
"To enforce moral limits and make sure everything goes smoothly."
"We are not madmen. We are not doing something that would destroy us."
The page is torn and ends here.
The name WelkinWeb rings a distant bell, but you can't quite place it.
Though you're certain it's nothing good.
